title: Angophora lanceolata
taxonomy: Angophora lanceolata is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Myrtaceae. It belongs to the genus Angophora, which is part of the order Myrtales. Further classification places the species in the subclass Rosidae and the clade Angiosperms. This classification aligns with the plant's characteristics and genetic relationships. The classification of Angophora lanceolata reflects its evolutionary history and phylogenetic position within the plant kingdom.

intro: Angophora lanceolata, commonly known as the Broad-leaved Apple or Smooth-barked Apple, is a species of flowering plant in the family Myrtaceae, native to Australia. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues, due to its antiviral, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Angophora lanceolata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support immune function and alleviate respiratory symptoms.
